concept: Field / Open Cultivated Land / Area of Activity
description: An area of open land, especially one used for cultivation or pasture; a domain of activity. 'Kosha' names the working expanse—land prepared for growing, space dedicated to purpose.
sound_symbolism: The word spreads openly. 'Ko' marks with definition, field bounded. Then 'shao' /ʃa/ extends across, open space continuing.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo kosha' (the field). Can refer to agricultural land or metaphorical domains of work or study.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Fields feed communities—cultivated land that produces food through careful tending.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Fields represented collective work—land that required community effort to prepare and maintain.
